head.hi: Sir Julian Pauncefote to Mr. Gresham.

div.p: Sir: I have the honor, in accordance with instructions received from the Earl of Kimberley, to inclose the substance of a telegram, dated the 22d ultimo, which was received by his lordship on the 31st ultimo from Her Majesty’s consul at Apia, reporting the outbreak of hostilities in Samoa.

opener.seg: [Paraphrase of telegram from Consul Cusack-Smith, Samoa, March 22, 1894, received March 31.]

frus:attachment.p: Hostilities have been in progress since the 10th instant. Hitherto the Government troops have been successful. The foreign consuls have undertaken to mediate. An armistice has just been proclaimed and a satisfactory peace is expected to follow. It is not at all necessary to send a man-of-war to Samoa.
